canonical_truth.summary: YY meets Tock the woodpecker on the stone wall during a hungry late-winter morning; Tock tells YY about a brewing fair-trade conversation among the neighbors.
canonical_truth.concrete_outcome: YY learns about a brewing inter-neighbor conversation regarding fair trades during late-winter scarcity; he has not yet committed to participating.

perception_prompt.noticeable_surface: Tock perched on the stone wall, fluffed against the cold, full of news — neighbors are starting to talk about fair-trade norms now that late winter has thinned everyone.

authorial_note.real_world_inspiration: International Workers' Day rallies worldwide on 2026-05-01, with the global theme 'Ensuring Safety and Health at Work in a Changing Climate' — workers gathering to negotiate fairness as conditions shift.
authorial_note.translation_logic: May Day labor solidarity → anthropomorphic neighbors gathering to negotiate trade norms in a hungry late winter. YY meets Tock first because Tock is the world's information layer; this lands the social register for both branches without a full meeting yet.
authorial_note.restraint_reason: Day 1 establishes register; full social meetings stay off-screen for now. The news arrives through one neighbor in one encounter.
